title: Temporarily suppress player-owned interaction colliders while button 5 is held
summary: 0.6.33 keeps the simple button 5 rule from 0.6.32 and adds one more safe polish pass: while the override is held, the player temporarily disables its own interactive screen and control colliders so the screen stops behaving like a live target.
changes: Keep the current rule that button 5 disables player gaze and focus behavior while held.
Resolve the player-owned hosted interaction colliders for the screen and control surface.
Temporarily disable those colliders while the override is active, then restore their previous enabled states on release.
Leave playback, playlist, scene wiring, and the rest of navigation behavior unchanged.
reasoning: Official VaM grab-nav checks still involve UI-hit surfaces, so disabling only player focus was not enough to guarantee the screen stopped acting interactive.
The player already owns a distinct hosted control-collider seam, so temporarily suppressing that interaction layer is a cleaner route than adding more navigation-state logic.
This is the last safe grab-nav polish route before the seam would start getting too clever again.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: While button 5 is held, the player should stop presenting its own screen and control surface as interactive targets.
If that was the remaining blocker, grab-nav should feel freer while looking at the screen without adding more navigation complexity.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the queued post-navigation deadband yet.
This slice does not change slideshow, A-B policy, volume gestures, or spatial audio.
